title: Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 19, 2026 between Manchester City FC and Arsenal FC.

markets.question: Will Manchester City FC win on 2026-04-19?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 19, 2026
If Manchester City FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's 2-1 Premier League win over Arsenal at the Etihad Stadium cements trader consensus at 100% for their victory, propelled by Rayan Cherki's opener and Erling Haaland's decisive 65th-minute strike that erased Arsenal's lead and trimmed the Gunners' atop-table advantage to three points after 32 matches. Pre-match dynamics favored City with superior home form, a recent 2-0 Carabao Cup final rout of Arsenal on March 22, and Haaland's ongoing scoring threat amid Arsenal's travel fatigue. This outcome aligns with City's head-to-head dominance in recent clashes; no credible challenges like VAR reversals or disqualifications appear viable given official confirmation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's escalating injury crisis, highlighted by Gabriel Magalhães' fresh knee issue forcing him off in yesterday's FA Cup quarter-final loss to Southampton, has severely depleted their defensive options with William Saliba (ankle), Piero Hincapié (hamstring), and Mikel Merino (foot surgery) also sidelined, leaving Mikel Arteta short on center-backs for the April 19 Premier League title showdown at Etihad Stadium. Manchester City enter nearly fully fit after international breaks, boasting home advantage and momentum in the tight table race where Arsenal lead but face midweek Champions League demands against Sporting CP. Recent form shows Arsenal wobbling amid errors and absences, while City's squad depth positions them as trader consensus favorites despite historical tight head-to-heads.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the heat of a tight Premier League title race where Arsenal hold a slender lead atop the standings, trader consensus prices all halftime outcomes at 50% for the April 19 Etihad Stadium showdown, underscoring a fiercely competitive matchup with no clear edge. Arsenal's injury crisis has deepened post-international break and FA Cup quarter-final exit to Southampton, sidelining Gabriel Magalhães (knee), William Saliba (ankle), Martin Ødegaard (knee), and others like Bukayo Saka (fitness-managed) and Eberechi Eze (calf), testing Mikel Arteta's squad depth. Manchester City, fresh off their March Carabao Cup final win over Arsenal, boast home advantage and recent dominance in head-to-heads despite their own lingering concerns like Rodri's absence, keeping first-half dynamics evenly poised between cautious starts, City pressure, or Gunners counter-threats.

title: Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC - Exact Score
description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Manchester City FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for April 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC match originally scheduled for April 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's lead atop the Premier League table with 61 goals scored in 31 matches faces stern tests from Manchester City's chase, just three points back after 30 games, fueling trader uncertainty in this Etihad showdown critical to the title race. Arsenal's defensive injury crisis deepened post-international break and FA Cup exit to Southampton, sidelining Gabriel (knee), William Saliba (ankle), and others like Jurrien Timber and Eberechi Eze, while Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice remain fitness doubts—prompting makeshift backlines that could yield unpredictable scores. City, healthier after their 0-2 EFL Cup final win over Arsenal last month, boast strong home form and head-to-head edge in recent clashes, yet historical tight contests like multiple draws keep exact scorelines bunched in trader consensus.

title: Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC - Total Corners
description: Total corners markets for the Premier League game between Manchester City FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for April 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET.

markets.question: Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC: O/U 9.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Manchester City FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for April 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 10 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 10, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: In the Premier League title race, Manchester City host Arsenal at Etihad Stadium on April 19 in a pivotal clash, with trader consensus reflecting both teams' possession-dominant styles that frequently generate high corner counts through wide attacks and sustained pressure. Recent head-to-heads underscore this, including Arsenal's 11-1 corner edge in their September 2025 meeting (total 12) and 27 corners across their last five Premier League encounters versus City. Arsenal face mounting injury concerns—Mikel Arteta confirmed blows ahead of Bournemouth, with Bukayo Saka doubtful, Jurriën Timber sidelined, and others like Martin Ødegaard questionable—potentially curbing their crossing volume, while City assess a key fitness issue but hold a healthier squad. Expect intense rivalry dynamics, home advantage for City, and set-piece opportunities to drive total corners above league averages.
